conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Let me start by finding out about your situation.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children I'd like to ask about - Alex, who's 8, and Blake, who's 20.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. You have two children - Alex (8 years old) and Blake (20 years old).

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to gather some more information. Let me start with a question about your responsibility for the children.

Are you responsible for Alex and Blake? By this I mean, do they live with you and you're responsible for their care, or do you pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent) towards their upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live with me and I'm responsible for their care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to ask about Blake specifically, since they are 20 years old. Is Blake currently in approved education or training? This would typically be full-time non-advanced education (like A-levels or equivalent) or approved unpaid training that they started before turning 19.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now let me ask about your residency status.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake, or is there anyone else who might have an equal or stronger claim (for example, another parent or guardian who also cares for them)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else claims Child Benefit for them. I'm the only one caring for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me read through the official guidance to ensure I give you an accurate assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official UK government guidance, I can now provide you with an assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility:

**For Alex (age 8): ✓ Eligible**
You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. They meet all the criteria - they are under 16, live with you in the UK, you're responsible for their care, and no one else is claiming for them.

**For Blake (age 20): ✗ Not Eligible**
Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake. While Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 (under 20) if they're in approved education or training, Blake is 20 years old and not in education or training, so they don't meet the age criteria.

You can proceed to claim Child Benefit for Alex. If you have any questions about making a claim, please let me know!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16. You live in the UK (Manchester), Alex lives with you, you are responsible for their care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 (under 20) only if they stay in approved education or training. Blake is not currently in any education or training. Since Blake is 20 and not in approved education or training, they do not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex (age 8) as they meet all the standard criteria: under 16, living with you in the UK, and you're responsible for their care. However, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake (age 20) because they are over the age limit. Child Benefit can only continue past age 16 if the child is under 20 AND in approved education or training, which Blake is not.

Blake is 20 years old. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 (under 20) only if they stay in approved education or training. Blake is not currently in any education or training. Since Blake is 20 and not in approved education or training, they do not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex (age 8) as they meet all the standard criteria: under 16, living with you in the UK, and you're responsible for their care. However, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake (age 20) because they are over the age limit. Child Benefit can only continue past age 16 if the child is under 20 AND in approved education or training, which Blake is not.
